headline: Schwab rolls out teen investor accounts: What to know
summary: Charles Schwab (SCHW) Managing Director and Head of Investor Services, Jonathan Craig, joins Yahoo Finance anchor Julie Hyman to discuss the company's new teen investor accounts aimed at teaching teens to trade and learn investing with parental oversight.

headline: Investing Without Training Wheels: Are Unsupervised Teen Brokerage Accounts Really a Smart Idea?
summary: Introducing kids to stocks and investing at an early age has long been viewed as one of the smartest financial moves a parent can encourage. The math is straightforward and powerful: the earlier money goes to work in the market, the more time it has to compound into serious wealth. Time, after all, is the ... headline: 1 Financials Stock to Target This Week and 2 Facing Headwinds
summary: Financial institutions play a critical role, offering everything from consumer banking to wealth management and specialized financial solutions. But uncertainty about fiscal and monetary policy has tempered enthusiasm, and over the past six months, the industry has pulled back by 10.7%. This performance was worse than the S&P 500’s 1.3% fall.

headline: New Schwab account lets kids buy and sell stocks. Here's how.
summary: A new Schwab Teen Investor account allows kids to buy and sell stocks themselves, with parents as joint owners.

headline: Charles Schwab Debuts Teen Accounts—With a $50 Bonus for Kids Who Do Investing Homework
summary: The brokerage giant wants to help teenagers become better investors by giving them an early start and some extra education.

headline: Introducing the Schwab Teen Investor™ Account
summary: WESTLAKE, Texas, March 26, 2026--To give teens hands-on investing experience, Charles Schwab today announced the new Schwab Teen Investor™ account, a joint brokerage account for young people ages 13 to 17 and their parent or legal guardian.
